document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation planning, benefit-risk assessment, equivalence demonstration, PMS/PMCF evidence collection, and conformity with MDR general safety and performance requirements.
keyPoints: The plan states it is documented according to Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
Clinical benefits include reduction in patient dose compared with film screen systems and fast image acquisition time.
Clinical outcome parameters include DQE, MTF, linearity, recovery time, and image confirmation or acquisition time.
The plan uses literature studies, recall and adverse event searches, PMS data, PMCF, customer surveys, complaints, and imaging evaluation as clinical evidence sources.
The document states there is no common specification for the device.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation plan
concepts.evidence: This clinical evaluation plan is documented according to the Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation report
concepts.evidence: The results of the clinical evaluation and the clinical evidence on which it is based shall be documented in a clinical evaluation report which shall support the assessment of the conformity of the device.

concepts.name: General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.pageTarget: MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.evidence: The general safety and performance requirements that require support from relevant clinical data are identified.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose
concepts.evidence: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Clinical benefit
concepts.evidence: Clinical benefit 1 – a significant reduction in patient dose in comparison with the film screen system.

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency
concepts.evidence: Relevant clinical outcome parameters Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE)

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function
concepts.evidence: Relevant clinical outcome parameters Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) Modulation Transfer Function (MTF) Linearity

concepts.name: Equivalence demonstration
concepts.evidence: The equivalence evaluation should comply with MDCG 2020-5 clinical evaluation – equivalence, and analyze data published in the scientific literature to consider equivalence based on proper scientific justification.

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: The PMCF will be conducted as a part of the PMS activities.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: According to the PMS plan (A-PMS-5-001 Post-market Surveillance Plan), we will collect and utilize available information such as incident documentation, feedback and customer complaints, compile list of trend depending on collected data, scientific literature, and recall data.

evidence.claim: The document is a Clinical Evaluation Plan for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The covered product models are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models

evidence.claim: The plan is documented according to Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation plan is documented according to the Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: Revision 1 is described as a response to the first review by 3EC (NB), but the revision date is a placeholder.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 1 날짜 Response to 1st review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The target groups are adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended target groups Adult and children except infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Population

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is an indirect conversion flat panel detector for digital radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device is an Indirect Conversion Flat Panel Detector for Digital Radiography

evidence.claim: Clinical benefit includes reduction in patient dose compared with film screen systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 1 – a significant reduction in patient dose in comparison with the film screen system.

evidence.claim: Clinical benefit includes fast image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 2 – fast image acquisition time

evidence.claim: DQE, MTF, and linearity are clinical outcome parameters for the first clinical benefit.
evidence.evidenceText: The relevant clinical outcome parameter of the first clinical benefit described above is the detective quantum efficiency (DQE), modulation transfer function (MTF) and linearity.

evidence.claim: Recovery time and image confirmation time are clinical outcome parameters for the second clinical benefit.
evidence.evidenceText: For the second clinical outcome parameters are recovery time and image confirmation time.

evidence.claim: Performance requirements are measured and tested according to EN 62220-1-1.
evidence.evidenceText: These intended clinical benefits lead to performance requirements for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, which will be measured and tested in accordance with EN 62220-1-1.

evidence.claim: The plan states that there is no common specification for the device.
evidence.evidenceText: Common specifications There is no common specification for the device.

evidence.claim: The device complies with biocompatibility requirements according to EN ISO 10993-1 series.
evidence.evidenceText: The device complies with the biocompatibility requirements according to EN ISO 10993-1 series (A-TCF-5-144 Biocompatibility Assessment Report).

evidence.claim: The device complies with EN 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic compatibility.
evidence.evidenceText: The device complies with Electromagnetic Compatibility of medical electrical equipment according to EN 60601-1-2 (A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports).

evidence.claim: The device complies with EN 60601-1 for basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: The device complies with the basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment according to EN 60601-1 (A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports).

evidence.claim: Usability engineering is performed according to EN 62366-1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: In eliminating or reducing risks related to use error, usability engineering is performed in accordance with EN 62366-1:2015 during the design and development phase.

evidence.claim: The literature study uses PubMed, European Radiology, Medical Physics, and regulatory recall searches.
evidence.evidenceText: Scientific literature databases use PubMed, European Radiology and Medical Physics, and Internet searches use FDA Recall, MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sales and MHRA Alerts and Recalls for Drugs and Medical Devices to collect relevant data.

evidence.claim: PMCF will be conducted as part of PMS activities and customer survey and clinical evaluation data will be collected, analyzed, and reported every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMCF will be conducted as a part of the PMS activities. According to the PMCF plan (A-PMCFP-5-001 PMCF plan), customer survey data and clinical evaluation data will be collected, analyzed and reported every two years.

evidence.claim: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every two years.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present for the current revision; date fields show the placeholder '날짜'.
Source metadata authoritySignal is 'MFDS/domestic', but the document content primarily references CE MDR, Regulation (EU) 2017/745, and 3EC (NB).
Text contains extraction or drafting artifacts such as typographical errors and mixed Korean/English duplicated sections.
